title: cephalomanes grande
intro: Cephalomanes grande, also known as the Greater Tassel Fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the family Hymenophyllaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health. The plant's leaves and stems contain various compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, providing health benefits such as improved digestion and immune system function. In herbal preparations, Cephalomanes grande is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
